TSLA: operating margin

4 quarter(s) searched · 97 filing section(s) searched · 4 period(s) with evidence

Longitudinal Summary

Throughout the period, the company consistently highlighted improvements in operating leverage and achieved record operating margins. Gross margins for automotive and other segments showed varying trends, with some increases and decreases reported. Management emphasized a focus on operating margin and efficiency to drive profitability.

Forecasting Credibility: None

No guidance/actual pairs were found for operating margin, so forecasting credibility rating is set to null.

Trends

  • Consistent emphasis on operating leverage and efficiency
  • Varied trends in gross margins for automotive and other segments

Recurring Themes / Risks

  • Focus on maintaining strong operating margins
  • Importance of efficiency and cost reduction

Key Concerns

  • No specific guidance provided for operating margin to assess forecasting accuracy

Conclusion

Given the lack of specific guidance and actual comparisons for operating margin, it is not possible to evaluate the forecasting credibility of management in this area. The company's focus on improving operating leverage and efficiency suggests a strategic emphasis on maintaining strong operating margins, but without direct guidance, the actual performance against expectations remains unclear.

By Reporting Period

Q1 2022

What management said

  • "As a result of our ongoing improvements in operating leverage, we achieved a record operating margin of over 19%."
  • "Last quarter, we demonstrated a series of new financial records, including revenue, gross margins, operating margin and bottom line profitability."
  • "Q1 was once again a record quarter on many levels, by reaching the highest deliveries, profit and an operating margin of 19%."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • "As a result of our ongoing improvements in operating leverage, we achieved a record operating margin of over 19%."
  • "Q1 was once again a record quarter on many levels, by reaching the highest deliveries, profit and an operating margin of 19%."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A

+3 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment increased from 23.4% in the three months ended March 31, 2021 to 30.5% in the three months ended March 31, 2022, primarily due to the automotive gross margin impacts discussed above and an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive increased from 26.5% in the three months ended March 31, 2021 to 32.9% in the three months ended March 31, 2022."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for energy generation and storage increased from -20.4% in the three months ended March 31, 2021 to -11.7% in the three months ended March 31, 2022, primarily due to higher deployments of Powerwall which operated at a higher gross margin as well as an improvement in Solar Roof gross margin due to reductions in average costs per unit."

What management said

  • "Zachary Kirkhorn Despite these challenges, we were still able to achieve one of our strongest operating margins at 14.6%."
  • "On GAAP automotive gross margin, it declined sequentially to 27.9%."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

No actual results identified this period.

+3 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment stayed flat at 25.7% in the three months ended June 30, 2022 as compared to the three months ended June 30, 2021."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 28.4% to 27.9% in the three months ended June 30, 2022 as compared to the three months ended June 30, 2021."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment increased from 24.6% to 28.3% in the six months ended June 30, 2022 as compared to the six months ended June 30, 2021, primarily due to the automotive gross margin impacts discussed above and an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive increased from 27.5% to 30.6% in the six months ended June 30, 2022 as compared to the six months ended June 30, 2021."

OtherITEM 6. EXHIBITS

 

 

Tesla, Inc.

 

 

 

Date: July 25, 2022

 

/s/ Zachary J. Kirkhorn

 

 

Zachary J. Kirkhorn

 

 

Chief Financial Officer

 

 

(Principal Financial Officer and

Duly Authorized Officer)
Q3 2022

What management said

  • "Operating margin is one of our best yet, with improvements in operating leverage."
  • "Removing regulatory credits and Austin and Berlin, our operating margins would have been our strongest yet and auto gross margin would have been nearly 30%."
  • "We had our industry-leading operating margin reach 17%."
  • "So, the factories are running at full speed, and we’re delivering every car we make and keeping operating margins strong."
  • "On automotive profitability, our GAAP operating margin was 17.2% with automotive gross margin at 27.9%."
  • "And as Elon mentioned, we are continuing to build as many cars as possible while also maintaining strong operating margins."
  • "We also continue to experience margin headwinds associated with macroeconomic conditions, as we’ve discussed at length on prior calls."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • "Operating margin is one of our best yet, with improvements in operating leverage."
  • "We had our industry-leading operating margin reach 17%."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A

+4 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment increased from 26.0% to 27.4% in the nine months ended September 30, 2022 as compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2021, primarily due to the automotive gross margin increase discussed above and an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment decreased from 28.2% to 26.0% in the three months ended September 30, 2022 as compared to the three months ended September 30, 2021, primarily due to the automotive gross margin decrease discussed above and offset by an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 30.5% to 27.9% in the three months ended September 30, 2022 as compared to the three months ended September 30, 2021."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive increased from 28.6% to 29.6% in the nine months ended September 30, 2022 as compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2021."

OtherITEM 6. EXHIBITS

 

 

Tesla, Inc.

 

 

 

Date: October 24, 2022

 

/s/ Zachary J. Kirkhorn

 

 

Zachary J. Kirkhorn

 

 

Chief Financial Officer

 

 

(Principal Financial Officer and

Duly Authorized Officer)
Q4 2022

What management said

  • "Thus, there will be an impact on operating margin in the near term."
  • "The second comment I wanted to make here is that as a management team here, we're most focused on what our operating margin is."
  • "We believe we can keep pushing for strong volume growth while retaining the industry's best operating margins."
  • "And so as other areas of the business become more important, particularly the energy business, which is growing faster than the vehicle business and as we're heavily focused on operating leverage here, improving efficiency of our overheads, we think the right metric for us to be focused on is operating margin."
  • "Second, on cost reduction, we're holding steady on our plans to rapidly increase volume, while improving overhead efficiency, which is the most effective method to retain strength in our operating margins."
  • "So, in 2022, we delivered over 1.3 million cars and achieved a 17% operating margin, the highest among any volume carmaker, I think maybe among any carmaker."
  • "On a full year basis: revenue increased over 50%, operating income doubled, free cash flows increased over 50%, and our margins remained industry-leading."
  • "So based upon these metrics here, we believe that we'll be above both of the metrics that are stated in the question, so 20% automotive gross margin, excluding leases and rent credits and then $47,000 ASP across all models."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • "In 2022, we delivered over 1.3 million cars and achieved a 17% operating margin, the highest among any volume carmaker, I think maybe among any carmaker."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A

+4 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment decreased from 26.9% to 26.5% in the year ended December 31, 2022 as compared to the year ended December 31, 2021, primarily due to the automotive gross margin decrease discussed above, partially offset by an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 29.3% to 28.5% in the year ended December 31, 2022 as compared to the year ended December 31, 2021."

  • jump to section"The decrease in the year ended December 31, 2022 included utilization of $ 13.57 billion net operating loss carry forwards to offset our 2022 U.S. taxable income."
  • jump to section"We have determined that no significant limitation would be placed on the utilization of our net operating loss and tax credit carry-forwards due to prior ownership changes."
  • jump to section"Federal and state laws can impose substantial restrictions on the utilization of net operating loss and tax credit carry-forwards in the event of an “ownership change,” as defined in Section 382 of the Internal Revenue Code."
